app.title: binancetracker
app.tagline: Self-hosted P&L tracker for your Binance trades

dashboard.total_invested_help: Capital currently at work — sum of the average cost × quantity for each position you still hold. When you sell, the cost basis of the sold portion is removed.
dashboard.total_value_help: What your positions are worth right now, at the latest Binance price. It's quantity held × current price, summed across every asset.
dashboard.unrealized_pnl_help: The gain or loss you would lock in by selling everything right now at market price. Formula: Current value − Total invested. As long as you don't sell, this number is purely on paper.
dashboard.realized_pnl_help: The gain or loss you have already cashed in via past sells. Once a sale is done, the result is frozen here and doesn't move regardless of what the market does next.
dashboard.empty: No positions yet. Configure your Binance API key in Settings, then click Sync.

positions.quantity_help: How much of this asset you currently hold (sum of all buys + deposits, minus all sells).
positions.avg_cost_help: Weighted-average price you paid per unit (AVCO method). 2 BTC bought at 50k and 60k → average cost 55k.
positions.current_price_help: Latest known Binance price for this asset (refreshed periodically).
positions.unrealized_help: Gain/loss if you sold everything at the current price.

asset.lots_empty: No open lots. Try syncing to import your buys and deposits.
asset.lots_total_help: What you'd pocket (or lose) if you sold everything at the current Binance price. Now at = total market value, Cost basis = what you paid, P&L = the difference.

settings.title: Settings
settings.binance_section_help: Use a read-only API key. binancetracker never places trades.

add_fund.title: Add a fund manually
add_fund.unit_cost_hint: Leave empty to use the Binance price at the date above.
